body.title: agent_datacenter: message envelope — flex schema with rigid keywords
body.result: Envelope dataclass in bus/envelope.py. RIGID_KEYWORDS={from_device, to_device, sent_at, schema_version}. validate() raises ValueError on missing/empty rigid field. to_json/from_json round-trips. payload passes through unchanged. Envelope.now() convenience constructor.
body.description:
Define the message envelope schema. No transport yet — this is the data shape and validator that all bus messages use.

Decision (Akien 2026-04-27): Flex schema, not rigid 5-field lock. Rigid keywords are well-known conventions; payload is open. Devices advertise what keywords they emit via their capabilities() method.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/bus/envelope.py:
  RIGID_KEYWORDS = {'from_device', 'to_device', 'sent_at', 'schema_version'}
  @dataclass
  class Envelope:
    from_device: str
    to_device: str
    sent_at: str   # ISO 8601
    schema_version: str = '1.0'
    payload: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    def validate(self) -> bool: checks rigid keywords present and non-empty
    def to_json(self) -> str: ...
    @classmethod
    def from_json(cls, s: str) -> 'Envelope': ...
  Mirrors Igor memory shape: {id, metadata:{rigid keywords}, payload: open dict}

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/bus/envelope.py

Design rules:
- oop-first: Envelope is a dataclass with to_json/from_json methods — not a plain dict
- docs-in-code: RIGID_KEYWORDS set is the canonical source; module docstring explains why payload is open

Scope boundary:
IN: Envelope dataclass, validation, JSON serialization/deserialization
OUT: any transport (IMAP APPEND is T-adc-imap-server-embedded)

Test plan:
- Envelope(from_device='igor', to_device='CC.0', sent_at='2026-04-27T...').validate() returns True
- Envelope missing from_device raises ValueError on validate()
- to_json() / from_json() round-trips cleanly
- payload dict passes through unchanged
